preamble: The shift from the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the velar fricative [x] is an example of backing or retraction. This change involves moving the place of articulation from the postalveolar region further back to the velum.

examples.note: Old Spanish [ʃ] shifted to [x] in the Early Modern period.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
